VQ
Visual QA
Alpha Family · VLT Games
1
Game
2
Resolution
3
Screenshots
4
Options
5
Export
Step 1 — Game Selection
Path to the game resources directory
Full path to resources/{game_name}/ directory
Path must not be empty
Detected game
Step 2 — Resolution
Choose the resolution to validate
Resolution
The resolution determines which v/{resolution}/ and idata/{resolution}/ folders are read.
Step 3 — Screenshots (optional)
Provide screenshots for visual validation (LLM vision analysis)
ℹ Screenshots are optional. The primary analysis is static (JSON-based) and works without screenshots. If provided, an LLM vision step will confirm/deny findings and look for additional visual issues.
Screenshot of the game in idle state (no spin, no win)
Optional — screenshot from a different, correctly working Alpha Family game for layout comparison
Step 4 — Options
Configure severity, auto-fix, and rule filtering
Severity threshold
Only findings at or above this severity will be reported
Force gather
Rebuild game model
Re-read all resource files even if game-model-report.json already exists
Auto-fix
Auto-apply fixes
When enabled, fixes are applied without asking for each one
⚠ Auto-fix will modify game resource files directly. Make sure you have a backup or use version control.
Rule filtering (optional)
Comma-separated rule IDs to skip (e.g. A1, D1). Leave empty to check all.
Check ONLY these rules. Overrides skip if set.
Rules reference
All validation rules — [I] integrity, [C] consistency, [W] convention
Step 5 — Review & Export
Configuration is ready for the visual-qa workflow
Summary
Run command
✓ Copy the command above and paste it in your AI assistant (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.).
Full JSON config (for reference)
